title: T-072 — Docs gap: supplier_specs Phase 1 upload/view/review implementation brief
prompt: Write a self-contained engineering brief for supplier_specs Phase 1 per 03-TECHNICAL-PRD §5.5 + §15A.3. Output `design/specs/TEC-supplier-specs-phase1.md`. Cover upload, view, review and approval of supplier-provided raw material/component specs: document metadata, declared allergens/attrs, certificates, allowed supplier/item pair, cost/spec review blockers, lifecycle/review statuses, permissions, audit, API routes, UI list/detail/review modal states, RM usability hooks, PO/TO deviation behavior, and taxonomy distinction from customer_spec and factory_specs/internal_product_spec. Phase 1 is internal Technical-maintained master data; do not build supplier portal app code.

pipeline_inputs.details: The brief must be implementable by later schema/API/UI tasks without rediscovery. Include exact candidate routes, server actions, fields, validation states, permissions, events and test expectations. Mark supplier portal/external negotiation as Phase 2/out of scope.
acceptance_criteria: Brief states supplier_specs is Phase 1 internal Technical-maintained master data and distinct from customer_spec and factory_specs/internal_product_spec.
Brief defines upload/view/review flow including document metadata, status transitions, Technical approval/rejection, audit evidence and permissions.
Brief says PO/TO actual deviations create review tasks/events/non_conformance.requested and do not silently overwrite approved supplier_specs or BOM cost_per_kg.

Brief defines RM usability hooks: approved supplier, active approved non-expired supplier_spec, allergen compatibility, active item, cost/spec review not blocked, and QC/release status where configured.
Brief names candidate API routes/actions and UI states but does not implement app code.
risk_red_lines: Do not invent a supplier portal implementation for Phase 1.
Do not implement app code in this docs task.
